h2h_summary: Based on available data, Côte d'Ivoire and Norway have no recorded head-to-head meetings.
team_style_home: Ivory Coast under Emerse Fae play a dynamic 4-3-3 with a compact midfield block, quick transitions, and pace-driven wide attackers who are most dangerous immediately after winning the ball.
team_style_away: Norway play a direct, high-pressing 4-3-3 (shifting to 4-2-2-2) built around Haaland as a physical focal point, with Odegaard providing creativity between the lines and rapid counter-attacking transitions from a compact defensive shape.
wc_history_home: Ivory Coast are appearing at their fourth World Cup (2006, 2010, 2014, 2026), returning after a 12-year absence but have never advanced beyond the group stage.
wc_history_away: Norway have appeared at four World Cups (1938, 1994, 1998, 2026), with their best run being the Round of 16 in 1998 — where they famously beat Brazil 2-1 in the group stage — before a 28-year absence ended with qualification for 2026.
group_context: Round of 32 knockout — no draws; level after 90 goes to extra time and penalties. Winner advances to the Round of 16.
strengths_home: High-scoring attack — most goals in CAF qualifying with 25 in 10 games
Pace and creativity across wide positions with Amad Diallo and Yan Diomande
Excellent recent form including a 2-1 win over France in their final World Cup warm-up
strengths_away: Erling Haaland's elite goal-scoring and physical dominance in the box
Flawless World Cup qualifying record — 8 wins from 8, 37 goals scored
High-intensity pressing and explosive counter-attacking transitions
